blocks.body: Future Perfect: Time Expressions — by, by the time, before, by tomorrow evening, by next week, by then, already.

{"level":"B1–B2","intro":"Expressions like by, by the time, before, by tomorrow evening, by next week / month / year, by then, already often go with Future Perfect.","rules":[{"label":"By + future time","color":"emerald","text":"By shows the future limit before which the action will already be complete.","example":"By tomorrow evening, I will have finished the report."},{"label":"By the time","color":"blue","text":"This expression connects the completion of one action with another future event.","example":"By the time you arrive, we will have cooked dinner."},{"label":"Before / by then / already","color":"amber","text":"These words help stress the order of events and the idea that the result will already be ready.","example":"She will have left before the meeting starts. / By then, they will have already packed."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common time markers for this section.","items":[{"label":"Deadline","title":"by tomorrow evening / by Friday / by next year","subtitle":"By Friday, we will have sent everything."},{"label":"Future clause","title":"by the time you arrive / by the time the lesson starts","subtitle":"By the time you arrive, I will have left."},{"label":"Order","title":"before the meeting / before winter / before next month","subtitle":"They will have finished before winter."},{"label":"Result words","title":"by then / already","subtitle":"By then, she will have already learned the route."}]}

{"title":"2. How these expressions work","sections":[{"label":"by + a moment or deadline","color":"emerald","description":"By shows the future limit by which the action will already be complete.","examples":[{"en":"By tomorrow evening, I will have answered all the emails.","ua":"The work will already be ready by that moment."},{"en":"By next year, they will have built the bridge.","ua":"We look at a completed result by a future limit."}]},{"label":"by the time + another event","color":"blue","description":"By the time links Future Perfect to another future event or point.","examples":[{"en":"By the time you arrive, we will have finished dinner.","ua":"One action will finish before the other event."},{"en":"By the time the lesson starts, she will have printed the worksheets.","ua":"The result will already be ready before the other event begins."}]},{"label":"before + future point","color":"amber","description":"Before also helps show that the completion comes earlier than another future limit.","examples":[{"en":"She will have left before the meeting starts.","ua":"Her leaving will be complete before the meeting begins."},{"en":"We will have completed the roof before winter.","ua":"The result will be ready before another point arrives."}]},{"label":"already / by then","color":"violet","description":"Already and by then stress that the result will already be there by that time.","examples":[{"en":"By then, he will have already found a new job.","ua":"The result will already exist by that point."},{"en":"They will have already packed by tomorrow morning.","ua":"Already strengthens the idea of a ready result."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common combinations","intro":"See how time expressions connect with Future Perfect.","rows":[{"en":"By tomorrow evening, I will have answered all the emails.","ua":"by tomorrow evening","note":"a future limit"},{"en":"By next year, they will have built the bridge.","ua":"by next year","note":"a prediction of completion by a future limit"},{"en":"By the time you arrive, we will have finished dinner.","ua":"by the time","note":"one action will finish before another event"},{"en":"She will have left before the meeting starts.","ua":"before","note":"completion before another future point"},{"en":"By then, he will have already found a new job.","ua":"by then / already","note":"the result will already be ready by that moment"}],"warning":"After by the time or before in a future time clause, we usually do not use will: by the time you arrive, not by the time you will arrive."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["by + future time shows the limit before which something will already be complete.","by the time and before help show the order of two future events.","by tomorrow evening, by next week / month / year, and by then sound very natural with Future Perfect.","already strengthens the idea that the result will already be ready by that time."]}
